---
title: "TODO Random effects"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source('../assets/setup.R')
```

**"Random effects" is an umbrella term that covers both [random intercepts](rdint.html){target="_blank"} and [random slopes](rdslp.html){target="_blank"}.**

If we're modelling data which contains [grouping variables](groupstruc.html){target="_blank"} that contribute random variability to the data (see [Data generating process](dgp.html){target="_blank"}), then our model should include random effects.


worked example of moving from fixef line to ranef line

To get the group-level lines, we take the parameters of the fixed-effect line—the intercept and slope—and add on some adjustment value for each group to the intercept and slope (see [random effects](ranef.html)).



## Interpreting model-estimated random effects


- Interpret fixed effects first
- Ranefs only make sense relative to fixefs






## Extracting from fitted model

### Group-member-specific random effects


### Group-member-specific coefficients


### Variance components



::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
## Shrinkage

lorem ipsum

:::


::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
## Pooling

lorem ipsum

:::


## Linked flash cards

### Outgoing links

- TODO


### Backlinks

- TODO
